submission.author: Nan Liu
submission.name: Lower LR
submission.blurb: Same 9x512 SP-1024 KV4 tied-embedding baseline architecture with lower Muon/Adam learning rates (MATRIX_LR=0.02, SCALAR_LR=0.02, TIED_EMBED_LR=0.03). Systematic LR sweep showed default 0.04 was too high; optimal is ~0.02.

submission.author: Nan Liu
submission.name: 10L Mixed Precision
submission.blurb: 10-layer 512-dim model with lower LR (MATRIX_LR=0.02) and mixed int8/int6 compression: full int8 for first/last 3 layers, int6 (step=4 rounding) for middle layers 3-6. Fits 16MB via better compression while gaining an extra transformer layer over baseline.

PhiDelay Shared-Recurrent LM (3.23 bpb, 6.84MB)

submission.author: Bahman Masarrat
submission.name: PhiDelay-Inspired Shared-Recurrent Language Model
submission.blurb: Shared-recurrent PhiDelay-inspired language model with artifact-aware scaling and training-driven optimization under strict size constraints. Replaces self-attention with a lightweight multi-head delay mixer and reuses 3 physical blocks across 4 recurrent passes (effective depth 12) at dim=1024. Final int8+zlib roundtrip scored 3.2306 BPB under the 16 MB artifact cap.

Combines long-context training with training optimization to beat the naive baseline by 0.023 BPB.

submission.author: seanward
submission.name: Int6 MLP3x + MTP + Sliding Window Eval
submission.blurb: 7-technique stack: (1) int6 per-row quantization [-31,31] with zstd-22 compression enables (2) 3x MLP expansion (hidden=1536) while fitting under 16MB. Combined with (3) seq4096 training for long context, (4) MTP auxiliary head (training-only, 1 head at weight 0.01), (5) fp16 tied embedding passthrough, (6) sliding window evaluation (stride=512), and (7) co-optimized training dynamics (MATRIX_LR=0.02, MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, WARMDOWN_ITERS=3000). Mean sliding-window val_bpb across 3 seeds: 1.1625. MTP head excluded from artifact.

submission.author: vadim borisov (tabularis.ai)
submission.name: Int6 MLP3x + OrthoInit + SmearGate + BigramHash + SlidingWindow
submission.blurb: Six stacked improvements: (1) int6 per-row quant on MLP+attention with zstd-22, fp16 tied embedding + last-2-layer K passthrough. (2) 3x MLP expansion. (3) Tuned optimizer: matrix_lr=0.02, Muon momentum 0.99, warmdown 3000, grad clip 0.3. (4) Orthogonal + muP-scaled weight init (output projections scaled by 1/sqrt(2*num_layers)). (5) SmearGate + Bigram hash embedding. (6) Sliding window eval stride=64.

Non-record: Stacked hyperparameter tuning + eval2048 (RTX 5090, val_bpb 1.336)

submission.author: Gustav Welinder
submission.name: Stacked Hyperparameter Tuning + Eval2048
submission.blurb: Non-record (RTX 5090, 20 train shards): Stacked 5 hyperparameter fixes on the baseline 9x512 architecture. Key finding: WARMDOWN_ITERS=1200 is broken at 600s wallclock (fires from step 1); fix to 3000 plus MATRIX_LR=0.06, LOGIT_SOFTCAP=15, MUON_MOMENTUM=0.99, quarter-batch 131K tokens, and EVAL_SEQ_LEN=2048 together bring val_bpb from 1.362 to 1.336. Also tested butterfly MLP factorization, reservoir random MLPs, depth recurrence, and 6 alternative iso-byte shapes — none beat the tuned 9x512 baseline. 40+ total experiments via autonomous autoresearch loop.

pr.title: Non-record: Stacked hyperparameter tuning + eval2048 (RTX 5090, val_bpb 1.336)

submission.author: Renier Velazco
submission.name: FP16 Tied Embedding + LR/Warmdown Tuning
submission.blurb: Keep tok_emb.weight in fp16 during int8 quantization to eliminate the output-head quantization gap (0.007 -> 0.0005 BPB). Slightly reduce MLP hidden (992 vs 1024) to fit within 16MB. Tune warmdown (3600 vs 1200) and matrix LR (0.06 vs 0.04) for better convergence under the 10-min wallclock cap.

submission.author: Etai Zilberman
submission.name: BitNet b1.58: 12x768 MLP3x Ternary + Chinchilla Scaling
submission.blurb: 64.5M ternary-weight transformer (12 layers, 768 dim, MLP 3x) packed at 1.6 bits/param via base-3 encoding. Trains with STE ternary quantization and fp16 scale simulation for near-zero roundtrip gap (0.002 BPB). Beats 4-hour baseline in 10 minutes via Chinchilla-optimal scaling: 3.8x more params in same artifact size. No hyperparameter sweep, no eval tricks.
